title: Share of urban population with convenient access to an open public space
subtitle: Share of urban population within 400 meters of walking distance along the street network to an open public space.
caption:
Share of urban population with convenient access to an open public space

Share of urban population within 400 meters of walking distance along the street network to an open public space.

This choropleth world map displays the share of the urban population with convenient access to an open public space in 2022. The legend ranges from 0% to 100%, with color bands marking 10-percentage-point intervals from light yellow to dark green. Countries in the darkest green band, indicating shares above 90%, include Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and several nations in Western and Northern Europe such as the United Kingdom, France, and Germany. Russia and Turkey also appear in this highest category. In contrast, large portions of Africa and parts of the Middle East sit in the lightest shades of the legend, representing shares below 20%. Countries like Niger, Chad, and Sudan fall into these lowest bands. South America shows a mix of mid-range to darker greens, with Brazil and Argentina appearing in bands between 60% and 80%. The United States and Mexico sit in mid-range green shades, roughly between 40% and 60%. India and China appear in lighter green bands, generally between 20% and 40%. Several countries, particularly in central Africa and Greenland, are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data. The visual pattern highlights higher access levels across North America, Europe, and Oceania, while lower access levels are concentrated across much of the African continent and parts of Asia.
